niche: houseplant and office plant care service

pattern.imageEvidence: plant care specialist pruning and checking soil moisture on plant shelf

geometry.shadowRole: hero media and final contact only

pattern.styleContract: Fresh field-guide calm with soil-moisture proof, real plant maintenance evidence, sunlit action accents, and a guided plant-health story flow as the signature move.

tagline: Plant care for leaves that have meetings to attend.

hero.title: LeafDesk plant care hero image
hero.alt: A plant care specialist pruning and checking soil moisture on a lush indoor plant shelf in a bright office lobby with tools and watering can nearby.

contact.serviceArea: Recurring houseplant and office plant care, watering routes, pruning, soil moisture checks, yellow leaf cleanup, plant rescue triage, lobby plant refreshes, and vacation plant visits for homes, studios, offices, and sunny apartments

copy.eyebrow: Houseplant health consults and care routes
copy.heroText: LeafDesk reads light, soil feel, drainage, pruning needs, and plant stress before setting a care rhythm for calmer homes and offices.
copy.introTitle: Watering is only one line on the leaf report.
copy.introText: We check room light, soil moisture, pot drainage, leaf color, pest clues, humidity swings, and vacation gaps before deciding whether the next move is water, prune, rotate, wait, or rescue.
copy.servicesTitle: Plant support that starts with the room
copy.processTitle: How we read the room before watering
copy.quoteText: Send plant photos, room light notes, watering history, and any sad-leaf situations. We will reply with a care rhythm and first-visit plan.

services.title: Home Plant Route
services.text: Recurring watering, pruning, leaf cleanup, rotations, soil checks, and gentle notes for apartments, homes, and sunny shelves.

services.title: Office Greenery Care
services.text: Lobby, desk, conference room, and reception plants kept tidy with schedule notes that work around business hours.

services.title: Plant Rescue Visit
services.text: A focused triage visit for yellow leaves, dry soil, pests, drainage questions, and plants that are being loved a little too much.

process.title: Send plant photos
process.text: Room photos, plant count, window direction, watering habits, and problem leaves help us sketch the first route.

process.title: Check soil and light
process.text: We test moisture, inspect leaves, prune what is ready, clean surfaces, and adjust care by plant type and room conditions.

process.title: Leave care notes
process.text: You get plain notes about what changed, what to watch, and when the next visit should happen.

proof.label: triage for yellow leaves and sad corners

notes: Works: a guided plant-health story flow gives the page a memorable shape.
Proof: plant care specialist pruning and checking soil moisture on plant shelf. anchors the niche in the first viewport.
Action: Plan plant care is the main conversion path.

brandBrief.trustCue: soil, light, drainage, and leaf-stress proof.
brandBrief.accentBehavior: sun stays reserved for Plan plant care, active proof cues, and key booking moments.
brandBrief.imageProof: plant care specialist pruning and checking soil moisture on plant shelf.
brandBrief.avoidLookingLike: Avoid a generic lawn-route-status-board recolor; do not repeat stock hero framing, copied card rhythm, or vague local-service claims.

serviceDetails.turnaround: Care plan reply: plant photos, light, drainage, and watering history shape the first visit.
serviceDetails.whatToSend: Send plant photos, plant count, room light or window direction, watering history, pot and drainage notes, access needs, and any yellow leaf or pest clues.
serviceDetails.prepNote: Room photos, plant count, window direction, watering habits, and problem leaves help us sketch the first care plan.
serviceDetails.serviceRhythm: Calm-consult flow for recurring houseplant and office plant care: light, soil, drainage, access, plant count, and watering history shape a care rhythm before repeat visits begin.
serviceDetails.objectionAnswer: For houseplant and office plant care service, plant count, room light, watering history, drainage, access, and skip dates shape the first visit before a care cadence is set.
